required_preferences.fact: Prefers in-person check-ins with Denver-based colleagues, reserving video calls solely for the remote sales team

supersedes.fact: Uses only video calls for check-ins — avoids phone calls to foster stronger team connections

required_preferences.fact: Pauses all hiking and outdoor activity planning while restricted from exercise or walking for her back injury

supersedes.fact: Plans short local hikes twice a month — won’t drive more than an hour for outdoor activities

required_preferences.fact: Brings prepared lunches to eat in the office lounge Monday through Thursday — prefers not to eat at her desk.
Refuses all nonessential video calls or online social events on weeknights, prioritizing offline, low-stimulation activities for headache recovery
Uses voice-to-text and speech recognition software for all significant work communication, minimizing keyboard use except for brief tasks
Prefers organizing onboarding sessions in small groups instead of all-at-once webinars to foster deeper connections among new remote hires

persona: A 28-year-old office administrator supporting a remote sales team
